revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:凡河内国造]]
{{日本の氏族 |家名= Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko |家紋= |家紋名称= Unknown family crest |本姓= {{ill|Ōshikōchi clan|ja|凡河内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi clan|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395444}} |家祖= {{ill|Hiko Kosohori no Mikoto|ja|彦己曽保理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Kosohori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenson tribe|ja|天孫族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson tribe|1=Tenson-zoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10939350}}) |出身地= {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}{{br}}[[Ishikawa District]]{{br}}{{ill|Shiki District|en|Shiki District|qid=Q7496587|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District}} |根拠地= {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}{{br}}{{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}{{br}}{{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}{{br}}{{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}} |人物= [[Ōkōchi no Ajihari]]{{br}}{{ill|Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|en|Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|qid=Q385896|lt=Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|1=Ōshikōchi no Mitsune}} |支流= {{ill|Hiromine clan|ja|広峯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) }}
'''Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (凡河内国造, also written as 凡川内国造 or 大河内国造) refers to the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, or provincial rulers, who governed the region of {{ill|Ōshikōchi Province|ja|凡河内国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 凡河内国造|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395443}}.
== Overview ==
=== Spelling Variations ===
The name appears as '''Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Records|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Records|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}} section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}. Other spellings include '''Ōkōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Ōshikawachi no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry ===
* According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}’s "Kuni no Miyatsuko Records," {{ill|Hiko Kosohori no Mikoto|ja|彦己曽保理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Kosohori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. * The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} identifies {{ill|Ame-no-Takahikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Takahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940228}} as the ancestor of the Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan ===
The ruling clan, the '''{{ill|Ōshikōchi clan|ja|凡河内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi clan|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395444}}''' (also written as Ōshikawachi-uji), bore the {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}. Over time, they were elevated to {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}} in the 12th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (683 CE), later to {{ill|Imiki|ja|忌寸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imiki|1=Imiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4200269}}, and eventually to {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} for some members.
== Base of Operations ==
The clan's main stronghold was in the western {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} or central {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}.
=== Territory ===
The jurisdiction of the Kuni no Miyatsuko encompassed the area known at the time as {{ill|Ōshikōchi Province|ja|凡河内国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 凡河内国造|1=Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395443}}, corresponding to parts of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, and {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. However, within these provinces were various smaller districts, such as {{ill|Mino District|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chinu District|ja|茅渟県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ina District|ja|猪名県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shiki District|ja|志幾県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Konkuchi District|ja|紺口県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkuchi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mishima District|ja|三嶋県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggesting the Ōshikōchi clan did not control the entire area.
The clan’s position overseeing {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|qid=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}} likely enabled them to supervise {{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|qid=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin}} (immigrants from the Korean Peninsula and China) and play a key role in diplomacy for the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト政権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kingship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2658252}}.
== Deities Worshipped ==
The clan’s tutelary deity was possibly the {{ill|Kawachi Kunitsumi Shrine|ja|河内国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunitsumi Shrine|1=Kawachi Kunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11553387}}.
=== Related Shrines ===
* {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|lt=Zama Shrine|ja|座摩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坐摩神社}} ** Located in Osaka City, it is a prominent shrine in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} and claims the status of {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. The shrine's hereditary priestly family, the Watanabe clan, descends from the Ōshikōchi Sukune lineage.
== Descendants ==
* '''{{ill|Ōkōchi no Ajihari|ja|大河内味張|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi no Ajihari|1=Ōkōchi no Ajihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65258357}}''' ** A powerful landowner during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} who was punished for refusing to cede his lands to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|qid=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}. * '''{{ill|Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|en|Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|qid=Q385896|lt=Ōshikōchi no Mitsune|1=Ōshikōchi no Mitsune}}''' ** A renowned poet of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, he is counted among the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|en|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|qid=Q671039|lt=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}} and contributed to the {{ill|Kokin Wakashū|en|Kokin Wakashū|qid=Q1047167|lt=Kokin Wakashū|1=Kokin Wakashū}} alongside {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|qid=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}}. * '''{{ill|Hiromine clan|ja|広峯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** The hereditary priestly family of {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|qid=Q11484399|lt=Hiromine Shrine|1=Hiromine Shrine}} in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/816099 "御上神社沿革考 : 近江国野洲郡三上村鎮座"] by Haruji Ōta (1899). * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 "Genealogical Records of the Mikami Clan"], National Diet Library Digital Collections. * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301743/84 "Genealogical Records of the Kainoro Branch"], National Diet Library Digital Collections. * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991097/118 "Heavenly Records of the Deities (Sendai Kuji Hongi, Volume 3)"], National Diet Library Digital Collections. * {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|國史大辭典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420603}} (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan). * "Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary" by Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan). * {{ill|Shinto Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Daijiten|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590924}} (Rinsen Shoin).
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * {{ill|Ōshikōchi clan|ja|凡河内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi clan|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395444}}
{{令制国一覧}}
{{translated page|ja|凡河内国造|version=95196656|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:凡河内国造]]
<!--Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko--> [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== '''Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (凡河内国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, or regional governor, who ruled over the area known as "Ōshikōchi no Kuni" (凡河内国), corresponding to parts of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}, and {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}.
== Overview == === Name Variants === The title appears as "Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko" in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section. Other forms include "Ōkawachi no Kuni no Miyatsuko" (大河内国造) and "Ōkawachi no Kokuzō" (大河内国造).
=== Ancestry === * According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, '''{{ill|Hikokosoho no Mikoto|ja|彦己曽保理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokosoho no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was appointed as Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Jinmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. * The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} identifies {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} as the ancestor of the Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''{{ill|Ōshikōchi clan|ja|凡河内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi clan|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395444}}''' (凡河内氏), which bore the kabane {{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}. The clan was later elevated to the Ranks of {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}, {{ill|Imiki|ja|忌寸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imiki|1=Imiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4200269}}, and finally {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (683–685).
== Base of Operations == The clan's headquarters were located in the western part of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}} or the central region of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}.
=== Jurisdiction === The jurisdiction, known as "Ōshikōchi no Kuni," roughly corresponded to modern {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, and {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. However, it was not identical to these provinces, as the region included multiple administrative units such as {{ill|Mino-agata|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino-agata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chinu-agata|ja|茅渟県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinu-agata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ina-agata|ja|猪名県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina-agata|1=Ina no Agata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11571881}}, and {{ill|Mishima-agata|ja|三嶋県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-agata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Due to its control of modern {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|qid=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}}, the Ōshikōchi clan played a significant role in managing foreign visitors and conducting diplomacy on behalf of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト政権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kingship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2658252}}.
== Clan Deity == The clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} is speculated to be {{ill|Kawachi Kokutama Shrine|ja|河内国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kokutama Shrine|1=Kawachi Kunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11553387}}.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|lt=Zama Shrine|ja|座摩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坐摩神社}} ** Located in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, this shrine is an important {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims the title of {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} of Settsu Province. The Watanabe family, the shrine's caretakers, trace their lineage to the Ōshikōchi Sukune.
== Descendants == * '''Ōkawachi no Ajiwari''' (大河内味張) ** A powerful figure of the Kofun period who was punished for failing to offer tribute to Emperor {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|qid=Q241765|lt=Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}. * '''Ōshikōchi no Mitsune''' (凡河内躬恒) ** A Heian-period poet counted among the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|en|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|qid=Q671039|lt=Thirty-Six Poetry Immortals|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}}. Mitsune collaborated with {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|qid=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} in compiling the {{ill|Kokin Wakashū|en|Kokin Wakashū|qid=Q1047167|lt=Kokin Wakashū|1=Kokin Wakashū}}. * '''Hiromine clan''' (広峯氏) ** The {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} of the {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|qid=Q11484399|lt=Hiromine Shrine|1=Hiromine Shrine}} in Harima Province.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/816099 "On Kami Shrine Historical Examination"], Ōtani Jisaku, Meiji 32.2, National Diet Library Digital Collections. * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 Mikami Shrine Genealogy (Koma 84-97) / Various Genealogies, Vol. 28], National Diet Library Digital Collections. * 『國史大辭典』（Yoshikawa Kōbunkan） * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clan Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * 『日本古代人名辞典』（Yoshikawa Kōbunkan） * 『日本史広辞典』（Yamakawa Shuppansha） * 『神道大辞典』（Rinsen Shoten）
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * {{ill|Ōshikōchi clan|ja|凡河内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi clan|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395444}}
{{令制国一覧}}
{{translated page|ja|凡河内国造|version=95196656|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:凡河内国造]]
